data.title: Bitcoin Exchange Supply Falls To 6-Year Low — A Signal To Buy The Dip?
data.description:
Bitcoin’s price decline continues as the crypto market adjusts following its recent all-time high.   This has triggered renewed debate among investors: is this the ideal moment to buy the dip, or could more downside pressure still be ahead? Bitcoin Falls But Presents Opportunity Exchange balances for Bitcoin have plunged to a six-year, four-month low, signaling growing investor accumulation. Since the start of October, roughly 45,000 BTC—worth over $4. 81 billion—has been withdrawn from exchanges
source: https://beincrypto.com/bitcoin-exchange-supply-buy-the-dip-or-dump/

data.title: Russian exchange aggregator BestChange restricts ruble transactions for 95 platforms
data.description:
The largest Russian crypto exchange aggregator, BestChange, has imposed restrictions on the Russian ruble offerings of dozens of coin trading platforms. The move is in response to complaints about blocked bank cards from users of their services. These have been mounting after Russia took measures to curb financial fraud, which affected crypto trade as well. […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/russian-exchange-aggregator-bestchange-restricts-ruble-transactions-for-95-platforms/

data.title: Dogecoin Price Set To Go On A 2,000% Cyclical Surge To $4
data.description:
The Dogecoin price could be gearing up for an explosive move soon, as technical analysts suggest that the popular meme coin may be entering another parabolic cycle. While the broader crypto market declines, analysts believe Dogecoin’s historical patterns and price structures are setting the stage for a potential 2,000% rally that could see it soar as high as $4 by next year. Related Reading: Bitcoin Plunges To $105k As Investors Shift To Gold After Crypto Carnage Dogecoin Price To Mirror Pre-2017 Explosive Surge Crypto analyst Javon Marks has indicated that Dogecoin’s price action is closely mirroring the bullish setup that preceded its historic price rally in 2017. If this pattern continues, he predicts that the cryptocurrency may be preparing for its next cyclical surge to new all-time highs and beyond
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/altcoin/dogecoin-price-set-to-go-on-a-2000-cyclical-surge-to-4/

data.title: Wikipedia Traffic Drops as AI Answers Eat the Free Encyclopedia
data.description:

source: https://decrypt.co/344845/wikipedia-traffic-drops-ai-answers-eat-free-encyclopedia

data.title: Major US Bank Handing $5,000 To Customers in $19,500,000 Class Action Settlement
data.description:
A major US bank and its affiliates are preparing to hand millions of dollars to customers in a new class action settlement. Wells Fargo customers are expected to receive checks worth up to $5,000 this month. The payments will go to customers in California, stemming from a $19. 5 million settlement approved in May
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/10/18/major-us-bank-handing-5000-to-customers-in-19500000-class-action-settlement/

data.title: Stablecoin Risks and Opportunities Highlighted Amid Regulatory Developments
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, investors are advised to exercise caution when considering privately-issued stablecoins, which present risks similar to central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) along with their own unique challenges. Jeremy Kranz, founder and managing partner of venture capital firm Sentinel Global, emphasized the potential dangers associated with these digital currencies. Kranz referred to privately-issued stablecoins as 'central business digital currency,' noting that they possess surveillance, backdoors, programmability, and controls akin to CBDCs. He highlighted the ability of issuers like JP Morgan to freeze assets and restrict access under certain regulations, such as the Patriot Act.

Kranz further explained that overcollateralized stablecoin issuers, which back their tokens with cash and short-term government securities, face the risk of 'bank runs' if too many holders attempt to redeem their tokens simultaneously. Additionally, algorithmic and synthetic stablecoins, which maintain their dollar-peg through software or complex trades, are susceptible to counterparty risks and dependencies, including de-pegging due to volatility or flash crashes in crypto derivatives markets. Kranz stressed the importance of investors understanding the fine print and making informed decisions about the financial instruments they choose to hold.

The stablecoin market has seen rapid innovation, likened by Kranz to '10 black swan events,' indicating both opportunities and risks arising from disruptive technological progress. The market capitalization of stablecoins surpassed $300 billion in October, as reported by DeFiLlama, with current figures exceeding $307 billion. This surge in interest followed the passage of the GENIUS stablecoin bill in the United States, which elicited mixed reactions from lawmakers. Marjorie Taylor Greene, a U.S. representative from Georgia, criticized the bill as a CBDC Trojan Horse, suggesting it could lead to a cashless society and digital currency that might be weaponized by an authoritarian government. As the stablecoin landscape evolves, investors are urged to remain vigilant and informed about the potential implications of these digital assets.

data.title: OpenSea Confirms Q1 Launch for SEA Token With Half of Supply Allocated to Community
data.description:
OpenSea has officially announced that it will be launching its highly anticipated SEA token in the first quarter of the year. In this exciting development, the platform has revealed that a significant portion, specifically half of the total supply of the SEA token, will be allocated directly to the community. This initiative highlights OpenSea's commitment to fostering community engagement and participation, ensuring that users play an integral role in the future of the platform.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/10/18/opensea-confirms-q1-launch-for-sea-token-with-half-of-supply-allocated-to-community

data.title: An address transferred 6,500 bitcoins to a new wallet after being silent for 3 years
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, an address transferred 6,500 bitcoins to a new wallet after three years of inactivity. These bitcoins were originally received from Ivanov (Bilyuchenko), a Russian cryptocurrency entrepreneur accused of co-founding the BTC-e and WEX exchanges and helping hackers launder bitcoins stolen from Mt. Gox.

data.title: Fed's Mousallem: If employment faces more risks, it may support the path of another rate cut
data.description: Fed Chair Musallem said that if employment faces more risks and inflation is under control, he may support another rate cut, believing that there is limited room for further rate cuts before policy becomes loose.
